summary: Bocage terrain collapses long sight lines and forces a commander to rely on short-range observation and reports.
source_basis: The Battle of Carentan (8-15 June): Utah Beach to Cherbourg
simulation_implications: Visibility arc should narrow sharply in hedgerow country.
Buttoned-up penalties should matter more in bocage than in open terrain.
game_implications: The map should not be omniscient; hidden threats should remain hidden until observed or reported.
Terrain should be a communications problem, not just a movement penalty.
